heading: Establishment of commodity trust

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: To provide for a trust solely to meet emergency humanitarian food needs in developing countries, the Secretary of Agriculture (referred to in this section as the “Secretary”) shall establish and maintain a trust of wheat, rice, corn, or sorghum, any combination of the commodities, or funds for use as described in subsection (c).

children.heading: Commodities or funds in trust
children.label: b Commodities or funds in trust
A
children.text: wheat in the reserve established under the Food Security Wheat Reserve Act of 1980 as of ;April 4, 1996

children.label: B
children.text: wheat, rice, corn, and sorghum (referred to in this section as “eligible commodities”) acquired in accordance with paragraph (2) to replenish eligible commodities released from the trust, including wheat to replenish wheat released from the reserve established under the Food Security Wheat Reserve Act of 1980 but not replenished as of ;April 4, 1996

children.label: C
children.text: such rice, corn, and sorghum as the Secretary may, at such time and in such manner as the Secretary determines appropriate, acquire as a result of exchanging an equivalent value of wheat in the trust established under this section; and

children.label: i
children.text: under paragraph (2)(B);

children.label: ii
children.text: as a result of an exchange of any commodity held in the trust for an equivalent amount of funds from the market, if the Secretary determines that such a sale of the commodity on the market will not unduly disrupt domestic markets; or

children.label: iii
children.text: to maximize the value of the trust, in accordance with subsection (d)(3).

children.label: I
children.text: from producers; or

children.label: II
children.text: in the market, if the Secretary determines that the purchases will not unduly disrupt the market; or

children.label: ii
children.text: by designation by the Secretary of stocks of eligible commodities of the Commodity Credit Corporation.

children.label: i
children.text: with respect to fiscal years 2000 through 2023 from funds made available to carry out the Food for Peace Act ( et seq.) that are used to repay or reimburse the Commodity Credit Corporation for the release of eligible commodities under subsections (c)(1) and (f)(2), except that, of such funds, not more than $20,000,000 may be expended for this purpose in each of the fiscal years 2000 through 2023;7 U.S.C. 1691

children.label: ii
children.text: from funds authorized for that use by an appropriations Act; or

children.label: iii
children.text: from funds accrued through the management of the trust under subsection (d).

children.label: aa
children.text: that causes human suffering; and

children.label: bb
children.text: for which a government concerned has not chosen, or has not the means, to remedy; or

children.label: II
children.text: created by a demonstrably abnormal event or series of events that produces dislocation in the lives of residents of a country or region of a country on an exceptional scale.

children.heading: Event or series of events
children.label: ii Event or series of events
I
children.text: a sudden calamity, such as an earthquake, flood, locust infestation, or similar unforeseen disaster;

children.label: aa
children.text: a significant influx of refugees;

children.label: bb
children.text: the internal displacement of populations; or

children.label: cc
children.text: the suffering of otherwise affected populations;

children.label: III
children.text: food scarcity conditions caused by slow-onset events, such as drought, crop failure, pest infestation, and disease, that result in an erosion of the ability of communities and vulnerable populations to meet food needs; and

children.label: IV
children.text: severe food access or availability conditions resulting from sudden economic shocks, market failure, or economic collapse, that result in an erosion of the ability of communities and vulnerable populations to meet food needs.

children.label: I
children.text: to assist in averting an emergency, including during the period immediately preceding the emergency;

children.label: II
children.text: to respond to an emergency; or

children.label: III
children.text: for recovery and rehabilitation after an emergency.

children.heading: Procedure
children.label: ii Procedure
children.text: A release under clause (i) shall be carried out in the same manner, and pursuant to the same authority as provided in title II of that Act.

children.heading: Insufficiency of other funds
children.label: C Insufficiency of other funds
children.text: The funds and commodities held in the trust shall be made immediately available on a determination by the Administrator that funds available for emergency needs under title II of that Act ( et seq.) for a fiscal year are insufficient to meet emergency needs during the fiscal year.7 U.S.C. 1721

children.heading: Waiver relating to minimum tonnage requirements
children.label: D Waiver relating to minimum tonnage requirements
children.text: Nothing in this paragraph requires a waiver by the Administrator of the Agency for International Development under section 204(a)(3) of the Food for Peace Act () as a condition for a release of funds or commodities under subparagraph (B).7 U.S.C. 1724(a)(3)

children.heading: Processing of eligible commodities
children.label: 2 Processing of eligible commodities
children.text: Eligible commodities that are released from the trust established under this section may be processed in the United States and shipped to a developing country when conditions in the recipient country require processing.

children.heading: Exchange
children.label: 3 Exchange
children.text: The Secretary may exchange an eligible commodity for another United States commodity of equal value, including powdered milk, pulses, and vegetable oil.

children.heading: Use of normal commercial practices
children.label: 4 Use of normal commercial practices
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable consistent with the fulfillment of the purposes of this section and the effective and efficient administration of this section, the Secretary shall use the usual and customary channels, facilities, arrangements, and practices of trade and commerce to carry out this subsection.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall provide for the management of eligible commodities and funds held in the trust in a manner that is consistent with maximizing the value of the trust, as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: for the management of eligible commodities in the trust established under this section as to location and quality of eligible commodities needed to meet emergency situations;

children.label: B
children.text: for the periodic rotation or replacement of stocks of eligible commodities in the trust to avoid spoilage and deterioration of the commodities;1So in original. Probably should be followed by “and”.1

children.label: C
children.text: subject to the need for release of commodities from the trust under subsection (c)(1), for the management of the trust to preserve the value of the trust through acquisitions under subsection (b)(2); and2So in original. The “; and” probably should be a period.2

children.heading: Exchanges
children.label: A Exchanges
children.text: If any commodity held in the trust is exchanged for funds under subsection (b)(1)(D)(ii), the funds shall be held in the trust until the date on which the funds are released in the case of an emergency under subsection (c).

children.heading: Investment
children.label: B Investment
children.text: The Secretary may invest funds held in the trust in any short-term obligation of the United States or any other low-risk short-term instrument or security insured by the Federal Government in which a regulated insurance company may invest under the laws of the District of Columbia.

children.heading: Treatment of trust under other law
children.label: e Treatment of trust under other law

children.text: considered a part of the total domestic supply (including carryover) for the purpose of subsection (c) or for the purpose of administering the Food for Peace Act ( et seq.); and7 U.S.C. 1691
subject to any quantitative limitation on exports that may be imposed under section 4606 of title 50.3See References in Text note below.3

children.heading: Use of Commodity Credit Corporation
children.label: f Use of Commodity Credit Corporation

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to the limitations provided in this section, the funds, facilities, and authorities of the Commodity Credit Corporation shall be used by the Secretary in carrying out this section, except that any restriction applicable to the acquisition, storage, or disposition of eligible commodities owned or controlled by the Commodity Credit Corporation shall not apply.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Commodity Credit Corporation shall be reimbursed for the release of eligible commodities from funds made available to carry out the Food for Peace Act ( et seq.) and the funds shall be available to replenish the trust under subsection (b).7 U.S.C. 1691

children.label: i
children.text: the actual costs incurred by the Commodity Credit Corporation with respect to the eligible commodity; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the export market price of the eligible commodity (as determined by the Secretary) as of the time the eligible commodity is released from the trust.

children.heading: Source of funds
children.label: C Source of funds
children.text: The reimbursement may be made from funds appropriated for subsequent fiscal years.

children.heading: Finality of determination
children.label: g Finality of determination
children.text: Any determination by the Secretary under this section shall be final.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The authority to replenish stocks of eligible commodities to maintain the trust established under this section shall terminate on .September 30, 2023

children.heading: Disposal of eligible commodities
children.label: 2 Disposal of eligible commodities
children.text: Eligible commodities remaining in the trust after , shall be disposed of by release for use in providing for emergency humanitarian food needs in developing countries as provided in this section.September 30, 2026
